arts.ttl: Rule change call to get Coventry City man well-deserved international caps
arts.sum: Our sister title at WalesOnline report that it’s understood Welsh football chiefs are looking to persuade associations in England, Scotland and Northern Ireland to change the rules, which would allow players over the age of 18 that have been registered with a club in one of the home nations for at least five years to be eligible to represent that country. arts.ttl: EFL pundit issues stark Doug King warning to Coventry City boss Frank Lampard
arts.sum: So far King’s decision to bring in former Chelsea boss Lampard has paid dividends with the 46-year-old coach lifting the team from three points off the relegation zone to three off the top six play-off mix – earning admiration from Sky Sports EFL pundit Don Goodman who has done a U-turn in his thinking, having previously written off City’s chances of promotion this season. Frank Lampard has been warned that he needs to do “more than okay” and get Coventry City promoted under the "current regime."

arts.ttl: Why free agent signing Jamie Paterson has provided Coventry City with a 'massive lift'
arts.sum: The Sky Blues striker says the 33-year-old winger/forward has been a huge boost to the squad with his presence on and off the training pitch, and feels he’s already struck up a nice playing connection with the former Swansea City man, who he hopes will help him score a few more goals. arts.ttl: Confirmed: Frank Lampard completes his second Coventry City transfer
arts.sum: Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group. The 33-year-old Coventry-born forward has been training with the Sky Blues since just before Christmas after leaving MLS side Charlotte FC, with whom he’d been since August, having previously played for Swansea and Bristol City in the Championship.

arts.ttl: 'Dream come true' - Jamie Paterson opens up on love for hometown club Coventry City - CoventryLive
arts.sum: Explaining how he came to join the club for training last month, he said: “I was in America, and I came back, and I spoke with the Gaffer about coming into training and having a look and see where we feel I’m at and how I am. “It would always be an exciting time to be part of this club,” he said, “but that added bonus of having the opportunity to get promoted with your hometown team, I’m not sure what can beat that in your footballing career.”

arts.ttl: Sheffield Wednesday suffer injury blow to hugely influential pair ahead of Coventry City visit
arts.sum: Sheffield Wednesday look to be heading into Saturday’s game with Coventry City without two key players after defender Di’Shon Bernard has been ruled out for the season and influential captain Barry Bannan is currently nursing an issue that’s kept him out of the last two matches with ‘mystery’ surrounding the timescale for his return. arts.ttl: Wright and Sheaf injury update as Frank Lampard talks timescales for key Coventry City duo
arts.sum: Asked about expected time scales for when the player will be ready to play, whether it will take him two or three weeks to get his fitness levels up, the head coach said: “Yeah, there will be that now but once he’s back we hope it is quite quick. Asked if having Wright back has given the whole group a lift, Lampard said: “Yeah, I think so because of the level of the player.
